caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5-digit occupational mix includes oil-change/lube-shop occupations and is a disclosed proxy.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions exclude pre-existing tunnel, gate and kiosk automation that is not AI.
inputs.pi_dist: No trade-area-level supply, pricing or churn data are available for the target EBITDA band.
inputs.pi_moat: Mister is a scaled public benchmark, not evidence that a regional target has the same subscription density.
inputs.t50_years: No representative current AI-adoption survey for NAICS 811192 was located; this is a bounded judgment.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Do not equate kiosks, gates or tunnel equipment with AI adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is not evidence of a measured generative-AI adoption curve.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Validate owner age, real-estate ownership and format locally before underwriting.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is not a nationwide owner-survey result.
inputs.active_consolidators: Buyer activity differs substantially by region, format and real-estate ownership.
inputs.buy_mult: Published advisory benchmark, not confidential deal comps; EBITDAR and owned real estate require separate treatment.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit depends on local supply, membership churn and real-estate/lease terms; no multiple expansion is assumed outside the published range.
inputs.pricing_structure: Mister's 76% UWC sales share is a scaled-public-company benchmark, not an industry average.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $89,975 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 811192); class <5: 9,362 firms, avg revenue/firm $236,758 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $37,053 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 2,475 firms, avg revenue/firm $711,100 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $111,287 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 2,110 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,148,854 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $179,796 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,542 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,320,900 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $363,221 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 124 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,254,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,604,829 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 62 firms, avg revenue/firm $59,966,484 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,384,755 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 186

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI addresses a limited administrative and above-site layer. Physical washing, loading, maintenance and site presence remain the labor core; legacy tunnel automation is excluded.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 811192 ($6,105,051,106) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 811192 ($14,968,891,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5-digit occupational mix includes oil-change/lube-shop occupations and is a disclosed proxy.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions exclude pre-existing tunnel, gate and kiosk automation that is not AI.
inputs.pi_dist: No trade-area-level supply, pricing or churn data are available for the target EBITDA band.
inputs.pi_moat: Mister is a scaled public benchmark, not evidence that a regional target has the same subscription density.
inputs.t50_years: No representative current AI-adoption survey for NAICS 811192 was located; this is a bounded judgment.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Do not equate kiosks, gates or tunnel equipment with AI adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is not evidence of a measured generative-AI adoption curve.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Validate owner age, real-estate ownership and format locally before underwriting.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is not a nationwide owner-survey result.
inputs.active_consolidators: Buyer activity differs substantially by region, format and real-estate ownership.
inputs.buy_mult: Published advisory benchmark, not confidential deal comps; EBITDAR and owned real estate require separate treatment.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit depends on local supply, membership churn and real-estate/lease terms; no multiple expansion is assumed outside the published range.
inputs.pricing_structure: Mister's 76% UWC sales share is a scaled-public-company benchmark, not an industry average.
source_review.summary: Mechanics, digests, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The sources support a durable physical service with a narrow AI surface and active but format-dependent M&A; all material proxy, adoption, owner-age and valuation limitations are already disclosed, so the record is publishable with caveats.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS, Car Washes Definition
sources.what_it_supports: Defines 811192 as establishments primarily cleaning, washing and/or waxing automotive vehicles and lists detail, mobile and self-service examples.

sources.title: Mister Car Wash 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Scaled express-exterior formats, subscription economics, kiosks/team-member sales process, and 76% of wash sales from Unlimited Wash Club in 2025.

sources.title: 2025 Car Wash M&A Report
sources.what_it_supports: Confirms a current specialist car-wash M&A report built from proprietary data and transaction experience; detailed report contents are not treated as public disclosed comps.

sources.title: Car Wash Industry Market Update from Amplify
sources.what_it_supports: Reports the February 2025 agreement to sell Driven Brands' U.S. car-wash business to Whistle Express for $385 million.

sources.title: Car Wash EBITDA & Valuation Multiples — 2025 Report
sources.what_it_supports: Published Q1 2025 format- and EBITDA-band valuation benchmark table for car washes, including express exterior and full service.

sources.title: The Carwash M&A Picture in 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Post-2022 financing-cost and multiple-compression context for car-wash M&A.

title: Car Washes
